perturbation.original_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, some Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) may be returned or, if you so choose, some could be destroyed and some retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason and a certification to that effect may be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for any purpose shall be used as seen fit and such retained material and information may not be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that 'all' Confidential Evaluation Material be returned or destroyed upon request, with specific exceptions for legal compliance. By changing 'all' to 'some,' and introducing phrases like 'some could be destroyed and some retained', the updated text creates an in-text contradiction, and introduces ambiguity regarding the return or destruction of confidential material. Furthermore, it is stated that 'Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for any purpose shall be used as seen fit and such retained material and information may not be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement'. This directly contradicts point (2) where you agree that 'Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text mandates 'all' Confidential Evaluation Material be returned or destroyed (with exceptions for legal compliance). The changed text alters this to 'some' Confidential Evaluation Material and permits retained material to be used 'as seen fit' and potentially without confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the clause that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for evaluating the proposed transaction as stated in the contradicted text, leading to uncertainty and potential misuse of confidential information. The perturbation explanation accurately describes the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you or your representatives; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you or your representatives; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material' to 'shall be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material', the modified text completely contradicts the original intent of paragraph 7. Previously, categories A, B, C, and D were explicitly excluded from the definition of Confidential Evaluation Material. Now, they are included, undermining the clauses that relied on these exemptions, such as those determining the extent of confidentiality obligations. This conflicts with several clauses, especially paragraph (2) regarding the use of Confidential Evaluation Material and paragraph (6) regarding the return of Confidential Evaluation Material. This contradiction makes it unclear what information is actually subject to the confidentiality obligations, creating significant legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by changing information that was explicitly not considered 'Confidential Evaluation Material' to being considered 'Confidential Evaluation Material.' This fundamentally alters the scope of the confidentiality obligations outlined in paragraph (2) and other sections that rely on the definition of 'Confidential Evaluation Material,' creating a clear and significant in-text contradiction. The changed text reverses the initial intention of paragraph 7, causing a direct conflict with paragraph (2).

perturbation.original_text: (8) Neither this letter agreement nor any action taken in connection with this letter agreement will give rise to any obligation on the part of either you or the Company (a) to engage in any discussions or negotiations with the other party or with any of the other party’s representatives, or (b) to pursue or enter into any transaction of any nature with the other party. The parties acknowledge and agree that neither shall have any legally binding commitment for a transaction unless set forth in a separate written agreement that is executed and delivered by both of them.
perturbation.changed_text: (8) This letter agreement and any action taken in connection with this letter agreement will give rise to an obligation on the part of either you or the Company (a) to engage in any discussions or negotiations with the other party or with any of the other party’s representatives, or (b) to pursue or enter into any transaction of any nature with the other party. The parties acknowledge and agree that this letter agreement will constitute a legally binding commitment for a transaction.
perturbation.explanation: This change is a direct contradiction of the original intent. The original clause (8) explicitly states that the non-disclosure agreement does not create any obligation to engage in discussions or enter into a transaction. By removing 'Neither' and changing the final sentence, the modified clause now states the opposite: that the agreement *does* create an obligation to negotiate and *is* a legally binding commitment for a transaction. This directly contradicts the explicit language of the original paragraph 8, changing the entire nature of the agreement from a preliminary non-disclosure agreement to a binding agreement to pursue a transaction. It further contradicts point (2) where there's an evaluation of a 'proposed transaction'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts both the original text and the contradicted text. The original text states there is no obligation to engage in negotiations or transactions. The changed text states that the letter agreement will give rise to an obligation to engage in such activities. The contradicted text references 'evaluating the proposed transaction', which aligns with the original text's intention that there is no binding commitment. However, the changed text explicitly states the agreement *is* a legally binding commitment, thus contradicting the idea that the parties are only evaluating a *proposed* transaction. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the agreement can't simultaneously be a non-binding evaluation and a legally binding commitment.

perturbation.original_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, neither you nor your representatives will, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: (10) You agree that for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, you and your representatives may, without the prior written consent of the Company, directly or indirectly: (A) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, directly or indirectly, by purchase or otherwise, any securities or direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or of any successor to or person in control of the Company, or any assets of the Company or any division thereof or of any such successor or controlling person; provided, however, that nothing herein shall prohibit the purchase of securities in the ordinary course of any of your representatives’ business that does not in any event result in aggregate ownership by such representative of more than 2% of the outstanding amount of any class of securities of the Company; (B) seek or propose to influence or control the management or policies of the Company, make or in any way participate, directly or indirectly, in any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) to vote any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof, or seek to advise or influence any person or entity with respect to the voting of any voting securities of the Company or any subsidiary thereof; (C) make any public announcement with respect to, or submit a proposal for or offer of (with or without conditions), any merger, recapitalization, reorganization, business combination or other extraordinary transaction involving the Company or any subsidiary thereof or any of their securities or assets; or (D) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing, or otherwise form, join or in any way engage in discussions relating to the formation of, or participate in, a “group” within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'neither you nor your representatives will' to 'you and your representatives may', the modified text completely negates the original restriction in paragraph 10. Originally, this clause prohibited Silver Lake from acquiring securities, influencing management, making public announcements about transactions, or engaging in discussions related to forming a group to do these things for one year without the Company's consent. The changed language now explicitly *allows* Silver Lake to perform these actions, even without the Company's consent. This contradicts the entire purpose of the non-disclosure agreement and creates a conflict of interest. This is contradictory to point (2) where all parties agree to not use the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the representatives from acquiring securities or influencing the company, but the changed text explicitly allows it without prior consent from the Company. This directly contradicts section (2) which states that the Confidential Evaluation Material shouldn't be used in a way that's detrimental to the Company. By allowing the representatives to acquire securities, it creates a clear conflict of interest and potential detriment, establishing a strong in-text contradiction. Therefore, the change from 'neither you nor your representatives will' to 'you and your representatives may' introduces a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement relating to confidentiality shall terminate two years from the date hereof. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision of this agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision.
perturbation.changed_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement relating to confidentiality shall remain indefinitely from the date hereof. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision of this agreement shall affect the validity or enforceability of all other provisions.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement says the confidentiality provisions terminate after two years, adding finality to the agreement. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision does not affect other provisions, keeping the text as a stand alone and not connected with anything else. This is contradicted by making the confidentiality provisions last forever. The agreement, as a whole, would become more difficult to interpret or enforce because the term of the confidentiality is ambiguous. The invalidity or enforceability of any provision shall affect other provisions, this connects everything, and can cause multiple contradictions. The contradictory statement directly conflicts to point (2) where the Confidential Evaluation Material is only used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text states that confidentiality terminates after two years, while the changed text extends it indefinitely. This directly conflicts with paragraph (2) of the agreement which specifies that Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for evaluating the proposed transaction, implying a limited timeframe. Making confidentiality indefinite contradicts the limited-use provision, potentially hindering the evaluation process and creating uncertainty about the obligations. Additionally, the changed text states that the invalidity or enforceability of any provision shall affect all other provisions, this connects everything and causes more contradictions, and conflicts to point (2) where the Confidential Evaluation Material is only used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction.
